context: Checkpoint 2026-04-29 after continuing streaming lane with explicit generalization rule from user: anything not LLM-specific should be generalized for reuse. We separated generic SSE framing/buffering from LLM-specific provider delta parsing and AgentResponse projection. WASM remains useful as plugin isolation/packaging, but native Tractor owns target-agnostic transport/framing primitives where that has broader value.
current_tasks: Latest local unpushed commits include e0c086d8 refactor(tractor): lift sse primitives out of llm bridge; 27a70fdf docs(agent): distinguish generic stream primitives; aa3b84e5 refactor(tractor): split generic sse stream primitives; ebf95a99 docs(agent): note buffered stream chunk persistence; 6619003a feat(tractor): record buffered stream chunks; 70ec2b38 feat(tractor): persist buffered stream chunks; 3d7f21ca feat(tractor): build stream agent response chunks; 3164b068 fix(tractor): align stream chunk first sequence; plus prior stream bridge stack.

Generic SSE framing now lives in packages/tractor/src/streaming/sse_events.rs and is exposed via packages/tractor/src/streaming.rs as crate-level reusable primitives: SseDataEventBuffer and parse_sse_data_events. It is no longer under the LLM bridge path.

LLM-specific code remains in packages/tractor/src/host/wasi_bridge/llm_stream_events.rs: OpenAI/Anthropic delta parsing, LlmStreamTextChunkDraft sequencing, partial AgentResponse node building, and storing SSE-derived chunks into NativeSync.

complete-http-stream still uses buffered HTTP for now, but after receiving final bytes it can persist any SSE-derived text chunks as partial AgentResponse nodes, reporting stored_chunks and last_sequence. This is not live token streaming yet.
Sequence alignment fixed: first partial chunk is sequence 0 when no last_sequence exists, matching pi-agent streaming_chunks semantics. Subsequent chunks increment monotonically.
ROADMAP and ADR-053 updated to distinguish generic stream primitives from LLM-specific streaming layer.
User explicitly reminded: do not tie everything to LLM; if a primitive has broader value, generalize it for future reuse.
blockers: Real live incremental HTTP/SSE is still not implemented. complete-http-stream drains buffered responses first; streaming_reader_available() remains false.
Provider stream:true is still disabled because final response semantics for SSE streams are not fully solved and end-to-end partial persistence/harness proof is pending.
Disk remains tight but stable after clean:light: ~8.2GB free; Rust targets ~4.86GB. Avoid broad global builds.
No GitHub Actions run yet for the large local unpushed stack.
next_actions: Next slice should implement live incremental read behind complete-http-stream with tests using a fake SSE server or a Read-based pure helper. Keep generic byte/SSE frame handling in tractor::streaming; keep provider JSON/AgentResponse projection in llm_stream_events.

Before flipping streaming_reader_available() true, prove: provider stream:true request body, host route enforcement/credentials stay active, partial AgentResponse nodes are stored as chunks arrive, final response sequencing follows last stored partial, and clients can observe partials.

Consider adding a pure helper that accepts an iterator/Read of byte chunks, feeds SseDataEventBuffer, persists chunks incrementally, and accumulates final_body. This can be tested without network before wiring ureq reader.

Resolve final response semantics for provider SSE: current pi-agent parsers expect JSON final_body. Options: host synthesizes compatible final JSON from accumulated deltas; pi-agent handles stream-result summary; or final AgentResponse content is assembled from partials. Decide before enabling.
If pushing current stack: local validations passed; push then use gh run watch --exit-status. Do not manually poll CI.
open_questions: What non-LLM stream primitives beyond SSE framing should be generalized now vs later?
Should host store AgentResponse partials directly, generic StreamChunk nodes, or dual-write? Current implementation stores AgentResponse directly for compatibility.
Should OpenAI/Anthropic delta parsing be extracted to a shared crate used by both pi-agent and Tractor to avoid duplication?
How should final response JSON be represented for true SSE provider streams so existing pi-agent response parsing remains correct?
How should stream errors/cancellation be represented after partial chunks have already been stored?
key_decisions_pending: Final response semantics for provider SSE.
AgentResponse direct chunks vs generic StreamChunk/dual-write.
Shared parser extraction vs host-owned parser duplication.
When to push the current unpushed stack.
